title: Living in Frontenac, MO — Complete Guide
content:
Frontenac is a 2.8 square mile suburb in mid-St. Louis County (63131). One of Missouri's smallest yet wealthiest communities with 3,500+ residents. Median home price ~.61M (2025). Homes range K-.5M. Consistently ranks among America's most affluent zip codes and top 1% for median household income nationally.

Location: Between Creve Coeur and Kirkwood, bordered by Manchester Road and Lindbergh Blvd. Easy access to I-64/40, Downtown STL, Forest Park, Gateway Arch, Zoo, City Museum, Busch Stadium. Near Ladue, Town & Country, Westwood.

Housing: Primarily single-family on 1-3 acre lots. Few upscale villas/townhomes off Clayton Road, no condos or apartments. Private neighborhoods off Geyer, Clayton, Conway Roads with winding streets and custom estates. Luxury amenities: movie theaters, saunas, wine cellars.

Schools: Most in Ladue School District (ranked 2nd in state). 82% of 2022 grads attended 4-year colleges. ACT scores substantially exceed MO and national averages. Small southern area in Kirkwood School District. Private options: MICDS, John Burroughs, St. Joseph's Academy, Chaminade.

Shopping: Plaza Frontenac (Saks, Gucci, Louis Vuitton, Neiman Marcus, Tiffany). Dining: Brio Tuscan Grille, BrickTop's, Fleming's, Canyon Cafe. Also: Old Frontenac Square, Le Chateau Village, Frontenac Grove.

Recreation: Life Time Fitness (128K sqft, opened 2019). Frontenac Racquet Club (10 indoor tennis courts, nearly 50 years).

History: Presbyterian settlers 1832, Old Meeting House (MO's oldest Protestant stone church, National Register). John Snyder's 1857 Federal-style home on 250 acres. Incorporated as village 1947, city 1950.

Development: M Clark Family Branch library (74K sqft). Frontenac 2050 Plan for walkability and city park. Continuous sidewalks connecting Clayton Road and Kirkwood by end of 2024.
